skill.name: Azure AI Text Analytics SDK for Python
skill.description: Azure AI Text Analytics SDK for sentiment analysis, entity recognition, key phrases, language detection, PII, and healthcare NLP. Use for natural language processing on text.
skill.summary: Client library for Azure AI Language service NLP capabilities including sentiment, entities, key phrases, and more.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only documentation skill providing guidance on using the Azure AI Text Analytics Python SDK. No executable code is present. Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected 0 potential security issues. The skill is purely documentation with code examples for an external SDK. No prompt injection attempts detected.

content.user_title: Analyze Text with Azure AI Text Analytics
content.value_statement: This skill enables Claude to work with Azure AI Text Analytics for NLP tasks including sentiment analysis, entity recognition, and PII detection. It provides code examples and best practices for integrating Azure's language processing capabilities.
actual_capabilities: Perform sentiment analysis with opinion mining on text documents
Recognize named entities and link them to knowledge bases
Detect and redact personally identifiable information (PII)
Extract key phrases from text documents
Detect the primary language of text with confidence scores
Analyze healthcare text for medical entities and relationships
limitations: Requires Azure AI Language subscription and credentials
API calls incur costs based on Azure pricing
Network connectivity required for API requests
Maximum 10 documents per request in batch operations

use_cases.title: Customer Feedback Analysis
use_cases.description: Analyze customer reviews and feedback to determine sentiment and extract key topics for business insights.
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts and business intelligence teams

use_cases.title: Document Processing Pipeline
use_cases.description: Process large volumes of documents to extract entities, classify content, and detect languages automatically.

use_cases.title: Compliance and Data Protection
use_cases.description: Scan documents for sensitive information like SSN, emails, and phone numbers to ensure compliance.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Sentiment Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Azure AI Text Analytics skill to analyze the sentiment of these customer reviews: [insert reviews]. Show positive, negative, and neutral scores.
prompt_templates.scenario: Analyze single or multiple text documents for sentiment

prompt_templates.title: Extract Named Entities
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Azure AI Text Analytics skill to recognize entities in this text: [insert text]. List each entity with its category, subcategory, and confidence score.
prompt_templates.scenario: Identify and categorize named entities in text

prompt_templates.title: Detect Sensitive Information
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Azure AI Text Analytics skill to scan this document for PII: [insert document]. Show redacted text and list all PII entities found with their categories.
prompt_templates.scenario: Detect and redact personally identifiable information

prompt_templates.title: Healthcare Text Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Azure AI Text Analytics healthcare NLP feature to analyze this clinical note: [insert note]. Extract medical entities, their categories, and normalized forms.
prompt_templates.scenario: Analyze healthcare documents for medical terminology

output_examples.input: I had a wonderful trip to Seattle last week! The food was terrible and the service was slow.
output_examples.output:
Document 1: Sentiment: positive (Confidence: 0.89)
Document 2: Sentiment: negative (Confidence: 0.92)

Opinion Mining:
- 'trip' -> positive
- 'food' -> negative
- 'service' -> negative

output_examples.input: Microsoft was founded by Bill Gates and Paul Allen in Albuquerque.
output_examples.output:
Entities:
- Microsoft (Organization, confidence: 0.95)
- Bill Gates (Person, confidence: 0.98)
- Paul Allen (Person, confidence: 0.97)
- Albuquerque (Location, confidence: 0.91)

best_practices: Use batch operations to process up to 10 documents in a single API call for efficiency
Handle document-level errors separately from successful results to ensure robust processing
Specify the language explicitly when known to improve analysis accuracy and reduce latency
anti_patterns: Do not assume all documents in a batch will succeed - always check for errors in results
Do not send sensitive data without proper Azure credential configuration and network security
Do not ignore the confidence scores - low scores may indicate ambiguous or low-quality input

faq.question: What credentials do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need an Azure AI Language resource with either an API key or Entra ID credentials. Set AZURE_LANGUAGE_ENDPOINT and AZURE_LANGUAGE_KEY environment variables.

faq.question: How many documents can I process at once?
faq.answer: Azure Text Analytics supports up to 10 documents per request. For larger batches, split your data into chunks of 10 or fewer.

faq.question: What is opinion mining?
faq.answer: Opinion mining is aspect-based sentiment analysis that identifies specific targets (like 'food' or 'service') and their associated opinions within text.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for healthcare data?
faq.answer: Yes, the healthcare NLP feature supports analyzing clinical notes for medical entities, but requires the Healthcare API feature enabled in your Azure subscription.

faq.question: Is there an async version of the client?
faq.answer: Yes, use azure.ai.textanalytics.aio.TextAnalyticsClient for async operations, which is recommended for high-throughput scenarios.

faq.question: How does PII detection work?
faq.answer: The recognize_pii_entities method detects categories like SSN, email, phone, and returns both the original text with sensitive data redacted and the detected entities.
